metadata.name: Federal Retirement Systems Index
metadata.description: Comprehensive catalog of all federal government and uniformed services retirement systems. Includes systems fully modeled in this repository and reference-only entries for smaller or restricted-access systems. Covers defined benefit pensions, defined contribution plans, and special category provisions.
sources: GAO Report AIMD-96-6 — Public Pensions: Summary of Federal Pension Plan Data
Congressional Research Service R47084 — Federal Retirement Plans FAQ
Congressional Research Service 98-810 — FERS Benefits and Financing
Congressional Research Service R42631 — Retirement Benefits for Federal Law Enforcement Personnel
Congressional Research Service RL30631 — Retirement Benefits for Members of Congress
3 FAM 6110–6180 (Foreign Affairs Manual — Foreign Service Retirement)
notes: This index catalogs every known federal retirement system. Not all systems are fully modeled in this repository.
Each entry includes a 'repo_coverage' field indicating whether the system is 'fully_modeled', 'referenced', or 'catalog_only'.
Population figures are approximate and based on the most recent available actuarial reports.
Systems marked 'catalog_only' have data files that are either classified, not publicly available, or serve a population too small to justify full modeling.

fers.type: Three-tier: defined benefit + Social Security + TSP
fers.status: Active — covers all federal civilian employees hired after December 31, 1983
fers.special_category_applies_to: LEO, FF, ATC, nuclear materials couriers, CBP Officers, Congressional members/staff (pre-2013)
fers.cola: Tiered CPI (full if ≤2%, capped at 2% if 2-3%, CPI−1% if >3%)

csrs.type: Standalone defined benefit (no Social Security, no TSP matching)
csrs.status: Closed to new entrants since January 1, 1987 — declining active population, large annuitant base

csrs_offset.type: CSRS with Social Security integration — annuity reduced at SS eligibility
csrs_offset.status: Closed to new entrants — applies to employees who had CSRS coverage, broke service after 1983 for >1 year, and had 5+ years by January 1, 1987
csrs_offset.employee_contribution_rate_pct: 0.8% (difference between CSRS 7% and OASDI 6.2%) below SS wage base; 7% above
csrs_offset.offset_rule: Annuity reduced at age 62 (or later) by Social Security benefit earned during CSRS Offset service
csrs_offset.repo_note: CSRS Offset uses CSRS formulas with SS offset; noted in contribution rates file but offset mechanics not separately modeled

fsrds.full_name: Foreign Service Retirement and Disability System
fsrds.authority: 22 U.S.C. Chapter 52, Subchapter VIII, Part I; Foreign Service Act of 1980
fsrds.type: Standalone defined benefit (no Social Security, no TSP matching)
fsrds.status: Closed to new entrants — nearly extinct active population

fsrds_offset.full_name: Foreign Service Retirement and Disability System — Offset
fsrds_offset.status: Closed to new entrants — small legacy population
fsrds_offset.description: Same as FSRDS but annuity offset by SS benefit at eligibility. Applies to FS employees who had break in service.

fsps.authority: 22 U.S.C. Chapter 52, Subchapter VIII, Part II; Foreign Service Act of 1980
fsps.type: Three-tier: defined benefit + Social Security + TSP
fsps.status: Active — covers Foreign Service employees hired 1984 or later
fsps.cola: Tiered CPI (same formula as FERS) BUT payable at any age for immediate retirees

tvars.authority: TVA Act of 1933; TVA Retirement System Rules and Regulations
tvars.administering_agency: TVA Retirement System Board of Directors
key_features: Rule of 80: age + creditable service ≥ 80 for unreduced retirement
Social Security offset: small reduction (~$52.50/month max) for post-1955 service
Separate 401(k) plan with TVA matching (25% of up to 6% of pay) — NOT the Thrift Savings Plan
Two benefit structures: Original Benefit Structure (OBS) and Cash Balance Plan
COLA determined by TVARS Board — not automatic CPI-based
tvars.trust_fund_note: Unlike most federal pension funds, TVARS invests in marketable securities (not special-issue Treasury bonds)
tvars.repo_note: Unique quasi-federal system. Queued for potential future build pending plan document depth review.

federal_reserve_system.administering_agency: Federal Reserve System (Board of Governors / Office of Employee Benefits)
federal_reserve_system.type: Defined benefit pension + Thrift Plan (distinct from TSP)
key_features: No employee contributions required for the DB plan — fully employer-funded
Employees also participate in Social Security
Separate Thrift Plan (not TSP) with employer matching
Covers employees of Federal Reserve Banks, Board of Governors, OEB, and CFPB
federal_reserve_system.repo_note: Operates as a private employer pension. Plan documents available through Fed HR but not structured like other federal systems.

judicial_retirement_systems.description: Multiple small retirement systems covering federal judges and judicial officers

systems.name: Judicial Retirement System (Article III Judges)
systems.description: Article III judges may retire at full salary after meeting the Rule of 80 (age + years of service ≥ 80, with minimum 10 years). Senior status allows continued service at full pay.
systems.unique_feature: Retirement at full salary (not a formula-based annuity)

systems.name: U.S. Tax Court Retirement Plan
systems.description: Tax Court judges receive annuity after 15+ years of service

systems.name: Court of Federal Claims Judges' Retirement System
U.S. Court of Veterans Appeals Judges' Retirement Plan
Judicial Officers' Retirement Fund
systems.description: Covers bankruptcy judges and magistrate judges

systems.name: Judicial Survivors' Annuities System
systems.description: Survivor annuity program for federal judiciary — separate from FERS/CSRS survivor benefits

judicial_retirement_systems.repo_note: Total judicial system population in the low hundreds for active participants. Documented for completeness but not modeled.

comptrollers_general_retirement_plan.description: Separate retirement provision for the Comptroller General of the United States (head of GAO)

nonappropriated_fund_plans.description: Retirement plans for employees of nonappropriated fund instrumentalities (NAFIs)
examples: Army and Air Force Exchange Service (AAFES) Retirement Annuity Plan
Navy Exchange Service Command (NEXCOM) retirement plans
nonappropriated_fund_plans.note: These operate similarly to private-sector pension plans under ERISA. Not covered by FERS/CSRS.

uniformed_services_retirement.description: The United States has eight uniformed services. All share the same basic pay table and retirement formula structure, though funding sources differ.

eight_uniformed_services.note: Added to MRF by FY2021 NDAA to protect pay during shutdowns

eight_uniformed_services.service: United States Public Health Service Commissioned Corps (USPHS)
eight_uniformed_services.department: Department of Health and Human Services
eight_uniformed_services.retirement_fund: HHS appropriations — NOT the Military Retirement Fund
eight_uniformed_services.pay_processing: U.S. Coast Guard Pay and Personnel Center (PPC)
eight_uniformed_services.note: Officers only — no enlisted ranks. Same pay table and retirement formulas as DoD services. Vulnerable to missed payments during government shutdowns (missed October 2025 check). Legislative efforts ongoing to include in MRF.

eight_uniformed_services.service: National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration Commissioned Officer Corps (NOAA Corps)
eight_uniformed_services.retirement_fund: Commerce Department appropriations — NOT the Military Retirement Fund
eight_uniformed_services.pay_processing: U.S. Coast Guard Pay and Personnel Center (PPC)
eight_uniformed_services.note: Officers only — no enlisted ranks. Same pay table and retirement formulas as DoD services. Also missed October 2025 payment during shutdown.

shared_retirement_systems.applies_to: DIEMS September 8, 1980 through December 31, 2017 (who did not opt into BRS)
DIEMS August 1, 1986 through December 31, 2017 (who elected CSB at 15 years)
All uniformed services members entering on or after January 1, 2018; opt-in available for eligible members through December 31, 2018

special_category_positions_6c.description: Federal civilian positions eligible for enhanced FERS retirement provisions under 5 U.S.C. §8412(d)-(e), commonly called '6(c)' coverage. These positions receive the 1.7%/1.0% tiered multiplier, earlier retirement eligibility (50/20 or 25 at any age), mandatory retirement ages, and an additional 0.5% employee contribution.
special_category_positions_6c.authority: 5 U.S.C. §8412(d) (law enforcement officers and firefighters); 5 U.S.C. §8412(e) (air traffic controllers); various public laws for specific position expansions
special_category_positions_6c.enhanced_formula: 1.7% × high-3 × first 20 years of 6(c) service + 1.0% × high-3 × years beyond 20
special_category_positions_6c.cola_exception: COLAs payable at any age (not delayed to 62) for special category retirees

mandatory_retirement_ages.note: Mandatory retirement age can be extended in some cases; varies by agency

eligible_position_categories.definition: Employees whose duties are primarily investigation, apprehension, or detention of individuals suspected or convicted of criminal offenses

State: Diplomatic Security Service Special Agents (Civil Service 1811 — not Foreign Service 2501)
DoD: Military Criminal Investigative Organizations civilian agents

eligible_position_categories.definition: Employees whose duties are primarily to perform work directly connected with the control and extinguishment of fires
Employees of the FAA or DoD whose duties include active separation, control, and sequencing of air traffic
Employees of the Department of Energy whose duties include transporting and protecting nuclear weapons and materials
GS-1895 series employees whose duties include arrival/departure processing at Ports of Entry
eligible_position_categories.note: Separate from Border Patrol Agents (who have standard LEO coverage). CBPOs received their own enhanced coverage in 2008.

eligible_position_categories.category: Members of Congress and Congressional Staff (pre-2013)
eligible_position_categories.definition: Members of Congress and designated congressional staff hired before 2013
eligible_position_categories.note: Members and staff first covered by FERS in 2013 or later accrue at the standard 1.0% rate, not the enhanced rate

eligible_position_categories.agency: Supreme Court of the United States

special_category_positions_6c.repo_note: Enhanced multiplier and contribution rates documented in rates-annual.json and fers-contribution-rates.json. This index provides the most complete listing of eligible positions.

tsp.eligible_participants: All FERS, CSRS, FSPS, FSRDS, and uniformed services members
tsp.fers_matching: 1% automatic + up to 4% match (5% total government contribution)
tsp.uniformed_services_matching: BRS: 1% automatic + up to 4% match; Legacy systems: no matching

federal_reserve_thrift_plan.description: Separate defined contribution plan for Federal Reserve employees — NOT the TSP
federal_reserve_thrift_plan.eligible_participants: Federal Reserve Bank employees, Board of Governors employees, OEB, CFPB

tva_401k.description: Separate 401(k) plan for TVA employees — NOT the TSP
tva_401k.employer_matching: 25% match on up to 6% of employee contributions
